title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police shot a man reportedly experiencing a mental health crisis after he attacked them with a sword on Hemenway Street.
Officers and EMS were injured in the incident; the man died despite receiving aid after being shot.
The event is part of a series of violent incidents near the Northeastern University campus in recent weeks.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, from Hudson, NH, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her late godmother, Diana.
Diana passed away from brain cancer in 2024 and received treatment at Massachusetts General Hospital.
Dubois is fundraising for MGH's Cancer Care Equity program, supporting families with cancer treatment expenses.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A woman in her 40s died in a house fire at 80 Glendale Ave., Brockton early Saturday.
Two other residents were hospitalized following the blaze, which began in a second-floor bedroom.
The fire's cause remains under investigation but is not suspected to be suspicious, officials said.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was killed in a shootout with New Hampshire State Police in Raymond after allegedly shooting an officer.
Masse was wanted for firing at his family and injuring a Nottingham officer earlier on the same day.
Police set up a perimeter, and Masse was found in the woods where an exchange of gunfire occurred, resulting in his death.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon and her friend Peggy will run the Boston Marathon after both survived cancer.
Gannon, 47, from Canton, Mass., was diagnosed with breast cancer in February 2024 and underwent a double mastectomy.
Both women are running to support Mass General Hospital and celebrate survivorship and friendship.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'SNL' for the fifth time on April 4, 2026, joining the show's five-timers club.
Jack White was the musical guest, marking his fifth appearance on 'SNL', sixth including The White Stripes.
The episode featured a cameo-heavy five-timers sketch and an 'SNL'-ified performance of 'Seven Nation Army'.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown addressed past criticisms about his left hand, attributing issues to a major wrist surgery in 2021.
Brown's improved performance to the left coincides with recovering from a 2023 reinjury and subsequent treatments.
Averaging career-highs, Brown led the Celtics to a top-4 playoff seed, entering MVP discussions without Jayson Tatum.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo returns to Boston with the Padres, reflecting on his Red Sox broadcasting tenure which ended in 2015.
Orsillo, now with the Padres, appreciates that Red Sox fans still fondly remember him 11 years after his NESN departure.
Orsillo reunites with former NESN colleagues, sharing moments with Jim Rice and Tom Caron at Fenway Park.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora is using Roman Anthony as designated hitter due to defensive concerns in the outfield.
Anthony participated as DH against the Padres, contributing to the offense with a triple and other key plays.
Despite a fielding error, Cora praised Anthony's improved throwing effort and noted areas needing defensive improvement.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two runs over four innings with four walks and three hits in a 3-2 loss to the Padres.
Aroldis Chapman allowed the Padres' winning run in the ninth inning, pushing Boston to their sixth loss of the season.
Connelly Early has tied for the most strikeouts through six career starts in Red Sox history, matching Daisuke Matsuzaka's record.
